shared_properties: Six-frame projectile atlas structure and central travel alignment.
Readable silhouette inside the spell lane even during alpha fades.
Single leading core supported by a trailing aura.
differences: Frame size grows to 55x55 px for a heavier threat profile.
Tail becomes a smoky violet-black aura around the core.
Palette shifts to charcoal, violet, and pale rim light rather than orange heat.

reference_images.label: reflect_academy_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Academy hall color rhythm, duel staging, card layout balance, and warm-versus-cool lighting anchor.

reference_images.label: reflect_academy_player
reference_images.purpose: Player silhouette, costume layering, hand pose, and magical focus posture anchor.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Use SpriteSheetBundle for the dark projectile and let the aura motion live inside the atlas frames rather than a runtime shader.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load obstacles/projectile_dark_fly_6f_10fps.png through AssetServer and register the atlas under projectile_dark_fly_6f_10fps.
runtime_notes: Dark projectile keeps the same logical travel system as the fire projectile.
Aura motion should not require a post-process blur effect in runtime.
Large silhouette helps communicate the slow heavy threat tier in late rounds.

context.usage: Dark projectile used for slow heavy reads with a larger silhouette and blur-like aura at 240 px per second.

shape.silhouette: Dense core orb wrapped by a smoky aura with violet edge wisps.

specs.lighting: Pale rim light tracks the front edge while violet haze softens the trailing boundary.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 330x55 px with six 55x55 frames.
Core orb remains at least 22 px wide in every frame so the hit point stays obvious.
Outer aura fades below 20 percent opacity before touching the frame edge on the rear side.
